decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.16°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). Previous attempts to increase spreader width hit domain bounds, and conductivity was previously pushed too high. The current spreader conductivity is at the maximum bound (500.0), so material tuning is exhausted. The strategy shifts to geometric alignment: moving the heat spreader horizontally to better center it over the heat source (chip), reducing lateral thermal resistance without violating size or material bounds.

changes.reason: Shift the heat spreader rightward to align its center (0.795m) closer to the chip center (0.6m), improving direct heat extraction path. The move of 0.125m is within the 25% domain width limit (0.3m).

expected_effects: Reduction in peak chip temperature due to improved thermal coupling between the chip and the high-conductivity spreader.
More symmetric temperature distribution in the spreader layer.
risk_notes: Moving the spreader too far right may reduce coverage on the left side, potentially increasing resistance to the left boundary condition, though the primary goal is vertical extraction efficiency.
Ensure the spreader does not overlap with the chip vertically (y-coordinates are distinct, so this is safe).
